draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-scale color palettes (beiges, soft grays, warm whites) rather than cold corporate blues or vibrant tech branding.
facade.label: Warm neutrals
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward inviting, trust-building aesthetics; likely values psychological comfort in work tools.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers cooler, more minimal, or higher-contrast palettes; may want the tool to feel streamlined or professional-distant rather than cozy.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond positively to organic, earthy materials—they suggest trustworthiness and handcrafted care, offsetting the clinical feel typical of business software
facade.label: Warm wood tones
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward natural, lived-in aesthetics. Likely wants the tool to feel like a personal workspace, not a corporate system.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers crisp, minimal, or modern digital surfaces. May want the tool to feel efficient and contemporary rather than grounded or tactile.

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-scale color palettes (beiges, soft grays, warm whites) rather than cold corporate blues or vibrant tech branding.
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward inviting, trust-building aesthetics; likely values psychological comfort in work tools.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a friendly, approachable voice in their CRM tools rather than corporate jargon
facade.label: Casual & conversational
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, human-centered microcopy; avoids sterile B2B language; likely values personality in UI text, confirmations, and empty states
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers formal, business-standard voice; sees personality as unprofessional; wants clear, direct, no-nonsense communication

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer visual scanning of client overview over dense contact tables
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual parsing, prefers glanceable status indicators (e.g., last contacted, contract value) over spreadsheet-style rows. May want thumbnail-style layouts for client profiles.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants maximum information density, prefers compact tabular views, or needs advanced filtering/sorting that cards don't naturally support. Dense data view would be preferred.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer immediate data refresh over animated transitions when tracking active projects and payments
facade.axisTargeted: Responsiveness: snappy instant feedback vs fluid animated delays
facade.label: Instant updates
facade.acceptImplies: User values speed and immediacy in CRM interactions; tolerates jarring state changes if information arrives fast; likely works across multiple tools simultaneously and needs quick confirmation loops
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers softer, guided transitions; may find instant changes disorienting; values perceived smoothness over raw speed; might be managing fewer concurrent projects and can absorb slightly slower feedback loops

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers need persistent access to core sections (clients, projects, invoices) without losing context—a sidebar keeps these always visible and scannable.
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure: sidebar vs. top tabs
facade.label: Sidebar navigation
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent navigation, vertical information hierarchy, and desktop-first layouts. Likely prefers staying in one section while referencing others.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants screen real estate for content, prefers top-level tabs or breadcrumb navigation, or needs mobile-first design where sidebars don't fit naturally.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers perceive soft, rounded UI elements as more approachable and less intimidating than sharp geometric designs, reinforcing the 'human tool' positioning already suggested by the warm neutrals acceptance.
facade.label: Rounded corners everywhere
facade.acceptImplies: User values softness, gentleness, and approachability in interface geometry. A fully rounded design system (buttons, cards, inputs, containers) would feel right. Suggests preference for organic, non-corporate aesthetic.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer clarity through sharp definition, or finds excessive rounding juvenile or unclear. Could signal appetite for modern minimalism or structured, geometric hierarchy—tension worth probing next.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business tool or a supportive coach
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style preference
Whether user prefers numbers/metrics front-and-center vs. narrative summaries and insights—reveals if they're data-driven or story-driven for client relationships
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate transitions—reveals pace preference for a tool used dozens of times daily
